reasoning: The secular decline in South Korea's TFR (0.72 in 2023) is driven by deeply entrenched structural issues. Although there was a slight uptick in marriages in 2023-2024 due to delayed pandemic weddings, government projections still expected 2024 TFR to fall (e.g., to ~0.68). Rebounding above 0.72 in 2025 would require a major trend reversal. Furthermore, the December 2024 martial law crisis likely introduced political and economic instability, which typically delays family planning—and any conceptions following the December 2024 event would only yield births in late 2025 anyway, minimizing its mathematical impact on the 2025 average. Therefore, an increase above 0.72 is highly unlikely.
